claim: MCP prompts are predefined templates for AI interactions that users invoke to add context directly to the model's context window
actionable: Create MCP prompts to expose example queries that show users optimal ways to use your MCP server before the model decides tool calls

claim: MCP prompt completion allows dynamic parameters and autocomplete for parameterized templates with just a few lines of code
actionable: Implement prompt completion in your MCP server by adding a completion function alongside the prompt generation function

claim: MCP resources expose raw data or content that applications can process (e.g., for embeddings or retrieval-augmented generation)
actionable: Use resources to expose database schemas, file contents, or structured data that applications can index and process beyond just context addition

claim: MCP sampling allows servers to request model completions from clients, enabling cost control and recursive chaining of servers
actionable: Use MCP sampling so servers can call the client's configured model without needing their own API keys, maintaining user control over cost and privacy

claim: MCP roots allow servers to query which projects/directories the client has open, enabling context-aware operations
actionable: Use roots to let your MCP server know which directories to operate in when the client has multiple projects open

claim: MCP interaction model has three parts: prompts (user-driven), resources (application-driven), tools (model-driven)
actionable: When building MCP servers, classify each capability as user-, application-, or model-driven and use appropriate primitives

claim: Web-based MCP servers require OAuth 2.1 authorization and streamable HTTP for scaling similar to normal APIs
actionable: When building web MCP servers, implement OAuth 2.1 (OSC 2.0 with security cleanups) and support streamable HTTP for richer interactions

claim: Enterprises can integrate MCP using OAuth flows bound to centralized identity providers like Azure AD or Okta for SSO
actionable: Deploy internal MCP servers with OAuth 2.1 flows using your enterprise's identity provider so users authenticate seamlessly via SSO

claim: MCP elicitation (asking for user input) is a primitive shipping in the specification for agents to request information from users
actionable: Use MCP elicitation to have agents request clarification or input from users rather than proceeding with assumptions

claim: Asynchronous tasks and long-running jobs are being built into MCP primitives to support hour-long agent operations
actionable: Expect future MCP support for async task primitives to enable agents to run complex workflows over extended periods
